page: Alibaba Cloud ecs.g6a.8xlarge is a general-purpose virtual machine instance powered by the KVM hypervisor. It features 32 dedicated physical cores on an x86_64 architecture, utilizing the AMD EPYC 7H12 processor running at a base clock speed of 2.6 GHz. The instance is configured with 128.0 GB of system memory, providing a balanced allocation of 4.0 GB of RAM per core. It does not include local storage or dedicated GPU hardware, relying entirely on network-attached storage and a baseline network bandwidth of 8 Gbps.

Benchmark evaluations indicate a distinct performance profile. Single-core processing capability is relatively low, with stress-ng single-core CPU performance placing in the bottom 10%. However, multi-core execution scales to average levels, as demonstrated by stress-ng multi-core and PassMark CPU scores. Memory performance exhibits a similar divergence; while cached small-block read bandwidth is average, uncached large-block read and write bandwidths are weak, falling into the bottom 25%. Despite these memory throughput limitations, the instance maintains average performance in multi-threaded decompression, Redis SET operations, and static web serving throughput.

From a resource density perspective, the ecs.g6a.8xlarge offers a balanced vCPU-to-RAM ratio suitable for general-purpose enterprise workloads. The dedicated CPU allocation ensures consistent processing performance without resource contention. This configuration is appropriate for multi-threaded applications, web serving, database operations, and medium-scale enterprise applications that do not require high single-core performance or high-speed local storage.
description: Alibaba Cloud ecs.g6a.8xlarge is a general-purpose instance featuring 32 dedicated physical cores powered by an AMD EPYC 7H12 processor at 2.6 GHz on an x86_64 architecture. It is equipped with 128.0 GB of RAM, yielding a balanced 4.0 GB per core, with no local storage or GPU accelerators. Operating on the KVM hypervisor, the instance delivers 8 Gbps of baseline network bandwidth. Benchmark data reveals a contrast between poor single-core performance and average multi-core capabilities, alongside average multi-threaded decompression and Redis SET throughput. Uncached memory bandwidth is a notable bottleneck, scoring in the bottom 25%. This instance is best suited for multi-threaded workloads, static web serving, and database operations that benefit from dedicated CPU resources without requiring high single-thread speeds or local NVMe storage.
og_description: Alibaba Cloud ecs.g6a.8xlarge is a general-purpose instance with 32 dedicated AMD EPYC cores, 128 GB RAM, and 8 Gbps network bandwidth, delivering balanced multi-core performance for enterprise workloads.

tagline: A dedicated AMD EPYC instance offering balanced compute and memory resources for multi-threaded enterprise workloads and web serving.

128.0 GB of system memory providing a balanced 4.0 GB per core
8 Gbps baseline network bandwidth with KVM virtualization
Average multi-core CPU and multi-threaded decompression performance

Suitable for web serving, database operations, and general-purpose workloads
